name: Madras Chicken Curry
recipeInstructions:
1. Dust chicken pieces with flour.
2. Heat oil in a large sauté pan until very hot.
3. Cook chicken until browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
4. Sauté onion and garlic until tender.
5. Add curry paste, salt, and chili powder to the pan. Stir in yogurt and chopped tomatoes.
6. Return the chicken to the pan and coat it well with the sauce.
7. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 30-40 minutes.
8. Serve over rice (make two cups) with the following condiments: raisins, sliced bananas, chopped tomatoes, shredded coconut, chopped green onions, chopped peanuts, and chutney.
9. For the chutney, use the mango/habanero Island sauce from "Hot Licks" with rum in it.
Madras Curry Paste:
1. Mix dry spices in a bowl: ground coriander, ground cumin, black pepper, turmeric, dry mustard, chili powder, and cayenne.
2. Add crushed garlic, ginger, and enough vinegar to make a smooth puree.
3. Heat oil in a saucepan until hot.
4. Add the spice mixture and reduce heat, stirring constantly until the oil starts to separate from the spices.
5. Allow to cool, then place in a glass jar and store refrigerated.
